reasoning: Virgin Galactic had over $500 million in cash and marketable securities as of the end of Q2 2025 (June 30). In August 2025, the company guided for a Q3 2025 free cash flow of negative $100 to $110 million. Even with this high cash burn, the company would exit Q3 2025 with approximately $400 million in cash, providing sufficient runway well past January 1, 2026. Additionally, the company recently scheduled its standard Q3 2025 earnings call for November 13, 2025, which would be highly unusual if an imminent voluntary bankruptcy filing were underway. Furthermore, its major debt obligation—$425 million in convertible senior notes—does not mature until February 2027, alleviating any immediate risk of a technical default due to debt maturity. Thus, with only 52 days left between the knowledge cutoff (Nov 9, 2025) and the resolution date (Jan 1, 2026), an involuntary or voluntary bankruptcy is extremely unlikely. The probability is estimated at a floor value appropriate for unforeseen catastrophic risks.
